signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD NET LONG +56,800 (76.5% long) creates significant crowded-long unwind risk for AUD/USD, which closed 0.7049 Friday. The G10 scorecard highlights AUD as the weakest currency this week (-0.799% avg) against a strong USD (+0.558% avg), with a 1.357% spread reinforcing downside pressure. Any disappointment from the RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun is likely to trigger a sharp unwind.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Crowded Short Squeeze
signals.text: JPY NET SHORT -105,136 (29.5% long) creates extreme short-squeeze risk for USD/JPY, which closed 160.19 Friday. Scotiabank highlights elevated JPY weakness raising intervention concerns, while the 286bp USD/JPY carry advantage (SOFR 3.59% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to attract long positions. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 50% hike priced) on 16 Jun is the catalyst for resolving the yield gap and potential policy shift.
evidence: Japanese Yen: Weakness raises intervention concerns – Scotiabank

signals.title: EUR/USD — US-Iran Peace Deal & USD Directional Uncertainty
signals.text: EUR/USD closed 1.1573 Friday, holding steady as markets await clarity on a possible US-Iran peace deal, which is likely to introduce USD downside pressure. EUR NET SHORT -22,320 (44.1% long) suggests some room for a short covering rally if the USD weakens. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hike priced) on 23 Jul will determine if the hiking bias can overcome USD-specific event risk.
evidence: Dollar Little Changed as Markets Assess Odds for US-Iran Peace Deal
Euro holds steady against the US Dollar as markets await clarity on a possible US-Iran peace deal

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Price Weakness & Retail Extreme Short
signals.text: USD/CAD closed 1.3987 Friday, trading on the front foot as lower WTI Oil prices ($84.3, -3.90% 1D) weigh on the commodity-linked CAD. Retail sentiment shows an EXTREME SHORT bias (91% short) in USD/CAD, creating contrarian reversal risk if the pair continues its rally. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 Jul is the catalyst for any shift in policy divergence.
evidence: USD/CAD Price Forecast: RSI enters overbought territory as bears defend 1.4000

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold Bias & Elevated Volatility Risk
signals.text: GBP/USD closed 1.3407 Friday, with the BoE on hold at 3.75% and market bias for continued hold. Elevated GBP/USD implied volatility (IV=11.0% vs HV30=6.0%, VRP=+5.0%) indicates strong hedging demand ahead of the next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 18 Jun. Deutsche Bank notes UK growth resilience but softer inflation, which limits upside for GBP.
evidence: United Kingdom: Growth resilience but softer inflation – Deutsche Bank
